path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\01. Opening - Donkey Kong.mp3
description: The original DK arcade game doesn't have too much to work with but they made the 75m stage (for some reason) so we have this. I think it sounds tinny and shit.
title: Opening
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\02. Donkey Kong Country Returns.mp3
description: This game's main theme remixes the original arcade theme and it's alright, although there are much better themes from the game.
title: Donkey Kong Country Returns
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\03. Donkey Kong Country Returns (Vocals).mp3
description: What is going on with these god-awful vocals? Whose idea was it to do this? The original music is fine and didn't need this treatment.
title: Donkey Kong Country Returns (Vocals)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\04. Donkey Kong.mp3
description: I remember when this debuted on the Brawl website and I was so shook. What is this experimental take on the arcade music? I think it's cool that so many composers worked on this soundtrack and some of them were completely loopy making music like this. Great. That being said, I think this particular remix is pretty unlistenable.
title: Donkey Kong
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\05. 25m Theme.mp3
description: This is joke music and if somebody picks it against me in tournament then I'll get them banned.
title: 25m Theme
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

filename: 06. Donkey Kong & Donkey Kong Jr. Medley.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\06. Donkey Kong & Donkey Kong Jr. Medley.mp3
description: I totally blanked out this remix which is wild because I'm such a Donkey Kong fan. I think it sounds like children bashing instruments too loudly.
title: Donkey Kong / Donkey Kong Jr. Medley
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\07. The Map Page & Bonus Level.mp3
description: I love this remix so much! It contains so many great moment. I especially love the Map music, and then it has this great build up into the switch to Bonus level. I also love the drum break before the loop. It's just so groovy and fun. Love it.
title: The Map Page / Bonus Level
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\08. The Map Page & Bonus Level.mp3
description: This is a cute medley of two tracks from the original SNES game, which is fun, but why would you ever select this when that absolute banger remix of these tracks is right there.
title: The Map Page / Bonus Level
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\09. Jungle Level (64).mp3
description: One of the first major remixes of this classic track. I love that it has two distinct parts, I love that it's so moody, I love that it's kinda weird. There are better remixes of this here though.
title: Jungle Level
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\11. Jungle Level (Melee).mp3
description: Huge remix! Those drums kick things off in a big way, and then they went for this big band style which totally hits. When the party really starts about minute in, I love it. They take some liberties with the melody before it switches to the second part of the song which is great. Then it's unafraid to go into the moody part in a big way. There are even elephant noises.
title: Jungle Level
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\12. Jungle Level (Brawl).mp3
description: This is a very faithful remix of this track and honestly I think it's a bit boring compared to other ones. The Melee remix is right there. They removed this from Ultimate which I think is smart.
title: Jungle Level
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\15. Jungle Level Ver. 2 (Brawl).mp3
description: Our first taste of something different for this track, a rock-based remix! They tackle the moody part of the song very differently which I respect. I learned that the generic Ver. 1 of Jungle Level from Brawl was removed from ultimate and THIS remix was no longer named with the 'Ver. 2'. Honestly, good.
title: Jungle Level Ver. 2
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

filename: 16. Jungle Level Jazz Style (for 3DS & Wii U).mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\16. Jungle Level Jazz Style (for 3DS & Wii U).mp3
description: They really saw the existing four Jungle Level remixes and decided to add two more in this game. This goes for the loud, big-band vibe from the Melee remix, and features a neat ragtime section. This might be the only ragtime music in smash so that's cool. It also skips the moody part of the song.
title: Jungle Level Jazz Style
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

filename: 17. Jungle Level Tribal Style (for 3DS & Wii U).mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\17. Jungle Level Tribal Style (for 3DS & Wii U).mp3
description: I like that Donkey Kong has the 'tribal' jungle sound (amongst many other styles), I don't think any other smash character can bring that to the table. This rendition of the slower section might be the best, but I don't think the remix blows us away.
title: Jungle Level Tribal Style
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\18. Jungle Hijinxs.mp3
description: If the 5 remixes of this track weren't enough they put in the version from DKC Returns, which is alright. Nothing special.
title: Jungle Hijinxs
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\19. Swinger Flinger.mp3
description: Yet another version of this music, this time from Tropical Freeze. Now I'm willing to forgive it because anything that comes from the Tropical Freeze soundtrack is solid in my book. The saxophone, the build up at the start, the groovy second section. There's a lot to enjoy about this one.
title: Swinger Flinger
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\20. Ice Cave Chant.mp3
description: I love that Sakurai put this track in. It's just one of many stellar tracks from the original games. It feels a bit random but it's still a lot of fun.
title: Ice Cave Chant
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\21. Funky's Fugue.mp3
description: This song is a bop and vibe for sure, but it's also a short loop. Can't be picking it in a smash match for that reason. Also those impact hits are a bit much.
title: Funky's Fugue
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\22. Gang-Plank Galleon.mp3
description: Great remix of this banger final boss music. I love that this was used for all the trailers for K Rool. However I have to deduct points for the weird rap (?) part.
title: Gang-Plank Galleon
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\23. Gang-Plank Galleon (Brawl).mp3
description: I always felt that this remix was shit. I can't put my finger on exactly why but I never liked it. Thank god they made the new one.
title: King K. Rool / Ship Deck 2
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\24. Snakey Chantey.mp3
description: This level from later on in DKC2 is really cool because it sounds like Gangplank Galleon but morphs into a fun, jaunty, different tune. That being said, I don't think they pulled it off. This remix does nothing for me. For some reason they also throw into the original Gangplank Galleon as well.
title: Snakey Chantey
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong
original-game: Donkey Kong Country / Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\25. Stickerbush Symphony.mp3
description: A perfect, classic remix. Impossible to overstate the impact this remix had! It took arguably the best Donkey Kong music and transformed it into a breezy, emotional, symphonic masterpiece. It's just so fucking good. This appearing in Subspace Emissary was everything.
title: Stickerbush Symphony
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong
original-game: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\26. Crocodile Cacophony.mp3
description: Incredible remix that I regularly pick in tournament. The use of the original SNES strings in the opening? So good. It takes the classic sound and makes it sound modern. It's just so intense and good.
title: Crocodile Cacophony
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong
original-game: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\27. DK Rap.mp3
description: Incredible that they made this and put this in Melee! This total quirk from a bunch of Brits at Rare ended up with this wild remix and it just makes no sense but honestly, work. Tragically, from Brawl onwards they only had a cut version of this without Lanky Chunky or Cranky's parts, presumably because they couldn't get the voices. A scandal! But the original is great.
title: DK Rap
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\29. Battle for Storm Hill.mp3
description: I think they added this to Brawl just to have some kind of intense music for Subspace Emissary but it's pretty plain. Jungle Beat didn't exactly have much to offer.
title: Battle for Storm Hill
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\30. DK Jungle 1 (Barrel Blast).mp3
description: I remember buying Barrel Blast from Blockbuster for like 5 quid and yes, it's a terrible game. But honestly this theme is a banger. Don't know why they removed it in Ultimate!
title: DK Jungle 1
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\31. Boss 2 - DK - Jungle Climber.mp3
description: Wild that they included this music from a terrible and obscure DS spin-off. The music itself is inoffensive, the game it's from was offensive.
title: Boss 2 - DK: Jungle Climber
composers: Yuichi Kanno, Yoshikata Hirota, Takashi Kouga
contributors: Yuichi Kanno, Yoshikata Hirota, Takashi Kouga
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\32. Gear Getaway.mp3
description: Great remix! I'm so glad that they included this excellent music from the rocket barrel levels from Returns. Those sections were a great addition to the series and it's great to see them represented here. I love the big-band style that has been so synonymous with Donkey Kong in smash.
title: Gear Getaway
composers: Minako Hamano, Masaru Tajima, Shinji Ushiroda
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\33. Mole Patrol.mp3
description: Not sure why they included this boss theme from Returns. It's a variation of Gear Getaway. It's alright.
title: Mole Patrol
composers: Minako Hamano, Masaru Tajima, Shinji Ushiroda
contributors: Minako Hamano, Masaru Tajima, Shinji Ushiroda
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 04 - Donkey Kong (3DS, GC, N64, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (1999)\34. Mangrove Cove.mp3
description: A scandal that this is the only original music from Tropical Freeze here. David Wise came back to make his best ever soundtrack and they didn't include any of the biggest tracks. Oh well, at least there's Mangrove Cove, the first level in the game. It's a tropical island chill vibe which I enjoy. I'm just happy it's here.
title: Mangrove Cove
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 04: Donkey Kong
